name: Arkhe - Pneuma & Ousia
desc: A useful Fontainian mechanism for both exploration and combat that uses two opposing forces: Pneuma and Ousia

item_values.name: Pneuma, Ousia, and Their Mutual Annihilation

content.text: Arkhe is one of the main energy sources used in
alignment as a special attribute while
alignment. It consists of two opposing forces:
as a special interaction is useful in both exploration and combat.
There are two ways to get a
Traveler have an innate Arkhe alignment
Any character can pick up a

block and obtain the specific alignment for 60s. It will remain despite teleporting to a different location or swapping the deployed team, or until another block is picked up. Fontainian characters can use their specific attacks to immediately trigger the effect corresponding to their Arkhe with a fixed cooldown. They can also pick up either blocks regardless of their innate alignment.
Arkhe application with characters with innate alignment
Traveler can apply Arkhe when they use a specific attack dependent on the specific character. It can be Normal Attack (Ex:
When used according to the specifications, Arkhe is applied in a follow up attack that can deal Elemental DMG without Elemental application.
". They have a separate cooldown and do not require a target to be unleashed.

When a character picks up a

block, the next Normal / Charged / Plunging Attack that hits an Arkhe aligned object or any enemy will apply the stored energy without dealing additional DMG. Bow users can use an Aimed shot to apply the Arkhe.

Fixed Storage Device and Energy Transfer Device
Storage devices can provide energy to Transfer and Research Terminals. Some devices are infused with an Arkhe alignment and requires

content.name: Fixed Storage Device and Energy Transfer Device

content.text: This device will grant powerful buffs to the troublesome opponents faced by adventurers or bandits.
Attacking the core of the device with the opposite Arkhe alignments will trigger
reaction which will spawn waves of enemies to defeat, after which the core will take the opposite Arkhe alignment.
The process must be repeated until the anomalies are stabilized.
The mechanism can be found both on land and underwater.
can to apply Arkhe twice. Through
depending on the alignment of the
Even though Arkhe application deals elemental damage, it does not apply said element and are thus unable to trigger Elemental Reactions.
In Co-op mode, Arkhe will act independently and consider players separately.
block, only the player who collects it will receive the alignment and thus, the Arkhe will only trigger when the player with infusion applies it.
reaction will also not be triggered if different players collect the opposite alignments.
When the player picks up a
block and acquire Arkhe alignment, it can last for 60s when not applied, regardless of party deployment, active character defeat, and teleportation.
The player can take advantage of it to complete the puzzles.
The Steambird Special Edition III: Pneuma, Ousia, and Their Mutual Annihilation
There is a new kind of energy in Fontaine that can power the clockwork meka. Let's find out more together!
The Steambird Special Edition V: New Creatures and Enemies: Ashore
As you explore Fontaine, there are new creatures and enemies waiting to be discovered.
The Steambird Special Edition V: New Creatures and Enemies: Underwater
When exploring the waters of Fontaine, you will encounter new creatures and enemies. Let's learn more about them together~
The Steambird Special Edition VI: Gear and Clockwork Roundelay
Let's admire the Artificed Dancers' Icewind Suite together!
After the Version 4.2 update, Travelers will encounter a new enemy All-Devouring Narwhal.
